LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD SPENCER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE - THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATIONS AND REASONS FOR THOSE RECOMMENDATIONS IN THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM BRUCE HIGHMAN. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST ATTACH THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF JUDGE PRO TEM HIGHMAN. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, (DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT) - DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ONLY CLAIM THAT SUPPORTS CIVIL CODE 3294 PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS THE FRAUD CLAIM. THE CIVIL PENALTIES UNDER THE SONG-BEVERLY ACT ARE A DISTINCT REMEDY. (SEE CC 1794(C); KWAN V. MERCEDES-BENZ OF N. AMERICA (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 174, 184.) COUNSEL FOR GM IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: REBECCA TOVAR, CSR 14200, 415.551.3778, EMAIL: RTOVAR@SFTC.ORG. =(302/ACM)

DECLARATION OF MARLENE FLORES IN SUPPORT OF GENERAL MOTORS LLCS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH COURTS DISCOVERY ORDER REQUEST FOR PROSPECTIVE SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100067933) FILED BY DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD SPENCER'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURTS OCTOBER 25, 2018 DISCOVERY ORDER AND FOR PROSPECTIVE SANCTIONS OF 500 PER DAY. THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH COURT'S OCTOBER 25, 2018 DISCOVERY ORDER (THE "ORDER") AND FOR PROSPECTIVE SANCTIONS OF $500 PER DAY IS GRANTED AS MODIFIED BELOW. DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE UNTIL MAY 1, 2019 TO CERTIFY IN WRITING UNDER OATH ITS COMPLIANCE WITH EACH DISCOVERY REQUEST SUBJECT THE ORDER BY, AT A MINIMUM, IDENTIFYING EACH DOCUMENT BY BATES NUMBER OR OTHER DISCOVERY MATERIAL BY SPECIFIC IDENTIFICATION THAT RESPONDS TO EACH SAID DISCOVERY. AFTER MAY 1, 2019, TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT DOES NOT CERTIFY COMPLIANCE WITH EACH AND EVERY OF THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS SUBJECT TO THE ORDER, PROSPECTIVE SANCTIONS OF $500 PER DAY WILL ACCRUE UNTIL SUCH TIME DEFENDANT HAS COMPLIED WITH SAID DISCOVERY REQUESTS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JYJUDGEPROTEM@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JASON YURASEK, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL HARABURDA, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065, CHREPORTER@COMCAST.NET, REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts October 25, 2018 Discovery Order And For Prospective Sanctions Of 500 Per Day
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jessica Zamora, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Marlene Flores, Esq. 949-777-6032 of Erskine Law Group for Defendant through Court Call.
Court reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065, chreporter@comcast.net, Reported.

Parites stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF RICHARD SPENCER's Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts October 25, 2018 Discovery Order And For Prospective Sanctions Of 500 Per Day.

The Court adopts and modifies the tentative ruling. Motion to Compel Compliance with Court's October 25, 2018 Discovery Order (the "Order") and for Prospective Sanctions of $500 Per Day is Granted as modified below. Defendant shall have until May 1, 2019 to certify in writing under oath its compliance with each discovery request subject the Order by, at a minimum, identifying each document by Bates number or other discovery material by specific identification that responds to each said discovery. After May 1, 2019, to the extent Defendant does not certify compliance with each and every of the discovery requests subject to the Order, prospective sanctions of $500 per day will accrue until such time defendant has complied with said discovery requests.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065, chreporter@comcast.net, Reported. =(302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James H. Tarter, Esq., Strategic Legal Practices, Telephone 310-929-4900, appearing for Plaintiff; CourtCall
Marlene K. Flores, Esq., Wood Smith et al, Telephone 925-222-3400, appearing for Defendant Lendlease (US); CourtCall

Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

General Motors, LLC's demurrer to the fraud by omission cause of action is OVERRULED. The amended complaint alleges sufficient facts to invoke the discovery rule and therefore the claim is not facially time-barred. (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808; Amended Complaint, 42.) Plaintiff's allegations of exclusive knowledge and active concealment support the claim in the absence of a fiduciary relationship. (See Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651; Amended Complaint, 12-15 and 102.) The economic loss rule does not bar the claim because plaintiff pleads tortious conduct independent of the alleged breach of contract.

order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Zamora, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practices, 310.929.4900, appearing by CourtCall for the Plaintiff Richard Spencer;
Jonathan M. Shugart, Esq. of Erskine Law, 248.601.4499, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant General Motors, LLC.

DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

Defendant General Motors, LLC's motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. The only claim that supports Civil Code 3294 punitive damages is the fraud claim. The civil penalties under the Song-Beverly Act are a distinct remedy. (See CC 1794(c); Kwan v. Mercedes-Benz of N. America (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 174, 184.)

Counsel for GM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Rebecca Tovar, CSR 14200, 415.551.3778, Email: RTovar@sftc.org. =(302/ACM)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Zamora, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practices, 310.929.4900, appearing by CourtCall for the Plaintiff Richard Spencer;
Jonathan M. Shugart, Esq. of Erskine Law, 248.601.4499, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant General Motors, LLC.

DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

General Motors, LLC's demurrer to the Seventh Cause of Action - fraud by omission- is SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Even if the court assumes that Aguilar tolled the statute of limitations, under Falk v. Children's Hospital of Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1454, 1466-1468, the allegations at paragraphs 7-8 and 61 indicate that plaintiff was on inquiry notice and the claim is time barred. (See Code Civ. Proc., 338.) Plaintiff's delayed discovery allegations at paragraph 41 fail to allege what specifically gave him reason to suspect the fraud in 2017 as opposed to 2012. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, '[a] plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.' [Citation] In assessing the sufficiency of the allegations of delayed discovery, the court places the burden on the plaintiff to 'show diligence'; 'conclusory allegations will not withstand demurrer.'" (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.) As explained in Cansino v. Bank of America (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472:

Because the discovery rule operates as an exception to the statute of limitations, "if an action is brought more than three years after commission of the fraud, plaintiff has the burden of pleading and proving that he did not make the discovery until within three years prior to the filing of his complaint." [citation] To excuse failure to discover the fraud within three years after its commission, a plaintiff also must plead "facts showing that he was not negligent in failing to make the discovery sooner and that he had no actual or presumptive knowledge of facts sufficient to put him on inquiry." [citation]To that end, a plaintiff must allege facts showing "the time and surrounding circumstances of the discovery and what the discovery was." [citation] Conclusory allegations will not withstand a demurrer. [citation] The discovery related facts should be pleaded in detail to allow the court to determine whether the fraud should have been discovered sooner. [citation]

Plaintiff's general charging allegations and paragraphs 13-15 and 101-102 show a duty
to disclose based on GM's exclusive knowledge and active concealment. (See Heliotis
v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651.)

The economic loss rule argument does not apply if plaintiff can plead a viable fraud claim.
Counsel for GM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Rebecca Tovar, CSR 14200, 415.551.3778, Email: RTovar@sftc.org. =(302/ACM)
